declare float @llvm.fabs.f32(float) #0

define float @fabs_div_nnan_only(float %x) {
  %x.fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %x)
  %div = fdiv nnan float %x.fabs, %x.fabs
  ret float %div
}

declare float @llvm.fabs.f32(float) #0

define float @fabs_div_nnan_only(float %x) {
  %x.fr = freeze float %x
  %div = fdiv nnan float %x.fr, %x.fr
  ret float %div
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions fast flags and reassoc/ninf combination, but individual flag combinations like nnan-only are not explicitly covered

tests.source_program:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare float @llvm.fabs.f32(float) #0

define float @fabs_different_operands_mul(float %x, float %y) {
  %x.fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %x)
  %y.fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %y)
  %mul = fmul float %x.fabs, %y.fabs
  ret float %mul
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare float @llvm.fabs.f32(float) #0

define float @fabs_different_operands_mul(float %x, float %y) {
  %mul = fmul float %x, %y
  %fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %mul)
  ret float %fabs
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only covers fabs(X) op fabs(X) where operands are identical. This test uses different operands fabs(X) op fabs(Y) which transforms to fabs(X op Y) - a separate optimization path in the golden patch not mentioned in the summary.

declare float @llvm.fabs.f32(float) #0

  %x.fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %x)
  %y.fabs = call float @llvm.fabs.f32(float %y)
  %mul = fmul float %x.fabs, %y.fabs
  ret float %mul
}
